message.content:
You need to make the CMS Visual Editor (cms/index.php) fully mirror every editable section of two frontend pages. Read ALL relevant files before editing. The project root is `D:\Eigene Dateien\Webseiten\Maxiworx\`. --- ## CONTEXT `cms/index.php` has a Visual Editor with sections per page. Each editable "box" is wrapped in `boxOpen(id, [keys])` / `boxClose(id)`. A `field(key, tag, class, isTextarea, value)` call renders a view/edit toggle for one field. The goal: **every piece of visible, user-readable text in the frontend should have a matching editable field in the CMS editor, in the same order as it appears on screen.** --- ## PART 1: STUDIO PAGE ### 1A — Read `studio.php` first (to understand ALL hardcoded content) The current CMS studio section (cms/index.php lines ~1108–1181) has: - Hero box ✓ - Studio Intro box ✓ - 4 room cards (live-room, control-room, iso-booth, mastering-suite) — each only has: tag, title, sub, desc. Missing all the hardcoded content below. - Akustik box ✓ **What is HARDCODED in studio.php but missing from CMS editor:** **Live Room card** (studio.php ~lines 35–73): - 3 spec values: "68 m²" (Fläche), "3,8 m" (Deckenhöhe), "0,4–1,2 s" (RT60) - 5 gear tags: "Naturstein-Boden", "Variable Akustikpanele", "Isolated Floating Floor", "Tageslicht", "Naturgarten-Blick" - h3 "Atmosphäre" section title - the Atmosphäre paragraph text (lorem ipsum style) - 4 atm gear tags: "Organische Wärme", "Natürliches Licht", "Raumklang variabel", "Inspirierende Umgebung" **Control Room card** (~lines 76–101): - 3 spec values: "38 m²", "3,2 m", "0,3 s" - 4 gear tags: "Symmetrische Akustik", "Tiefton-Behandlung", "Erdfarbenes Interieur", "Gedämpftes Licht" **Iso Booth card** (~lines 104–129): - 3 spec values: "12 m²", "–65 dB", "0,2 s" - 4 gear tags: "Naturstein-Akustik", "Floating Floor", "Tiefton-Absorber", "Diffusoren" **Artist Lounge (mastering-suite) card** (~lines 132–158): - `mastering-suite.desc2` IS in studio.php with `data-cms-key` but is NOT in the CMS box's key list - 5 gear tags: "Küche & Catering", "Naturgarten-Zugang", "Übernachtung möglich", "Kreativ-Atmosphäre", "Rückzugsraum" **CTA section** (~lines 214–229): completely hardcoded, completely absent from CMS editor: - Title: "Starte dein Projekt mit einem
kostenlosen Erstgespräch" - Desc: "Wir besprechen gemeinsam dein Vorhaben, deinen Zeitrahmen und dein Budget — ganz unverbindlich. Wir melden uns innerhalb von 24 Stunden." - Button text: "Kostenloses Erstgespräch" ### 1B — Changes to `studio.php` Make these hardcoded elements CMS-driven using `c($c, 'key', $lang) ?: 'fallback'`: **Live Room** — add data-cms-key + c() to: - spec values: keys `live-room.spec1`, `live-room.spec2`, `live-room.spec3` - gear tags: `live-room.tag1` through `live-room.tag5` - Atmosphäre h3: `live-room.atm-title` - Atmosphäre paragraph: `live-room.atm-text` - Atmosphäre gear tags: `live-room.atm-tag1` through `live-room.atm-tag4` **Control Room** — add: - spec values: `control-room.spec1`, `control-room.spec2`, `control-room.spec3` - gear tags: `control-room.tag1` through `control-room.tag4` **Iso Booth** — add: - spec values: `iso-booth.spec1`, `iso-booth.spec2`, `iso-booth.spec3` - gear tags: `iso-booth.tag1` through `iso-booth.tag4` **Artist Lounge** — add: - gear tags: `mastering-suite.tag1` through `mastering-suite.tag5` (desc2 is already `c($c,'mastering-suite.desc2',$lang)` in the frontend) **CTA section** — replace hardcoded strings with CMS fields: ```php // Title:

kostenlosen Erstgespräch' ?>

// Desc:

// Button: ``` ### 1C — Changes to `cms/index.php` Studio section **Replace the existing room cards loop** (lines ~1128–1151) with 4 separate explicit boxes, one per room, each containing ALL their content: ```php
``` Then keep the acoustics box as-is. **Add Studio CTA box** immediately after the acoustics box (before ``): ```php
``` ### 1D — Add $limits entries for all new Studio fields In the `$limits` array, after the acoustics limits, add: ```php // Studio room details 'live-room.spec1' => 15, 'live-room.spec2' => 15, 'live-room.spec3' => 15, 'live-room.tag1' => 40, 'live-room.tag2' => 40, 'live-room.tag3' => 40, 'live-room.tag4' => 40, 'live-room.tag5' => 40, 'live-room.atm-title' => 40, 'live-room.atm-text' => 400, 'live-room.atm-tag1' => 40, 'live-room.atm-tag2' => 40, 'live-room.atm-tag3' => 40, 'live-room.atm-tag4' => 40, 'control-room.spec1' => 15, 'control-room.spec2' => 15, 'control-room.spec3' => 15, 'control-room.tag1' => 40, 'control-room.tag2' => 40, 'control-room.tag3' => 40, 'control-room.tag4' => 40, 'iso-booth.spec1' => 15, 'iso-booth.spec2' => 15, 'iso-booth.spec3' => 15, 'iso-booth.tag1' => 40, 'iso-booth.tag2' => 40, 'iso-booth.tag3' => 40, 'iso-booth.tag4' => 40, 'mastering-suite.desc2' => 400, 'mastering-suite.tag1' => 40, 'mastering-suite.tag2' => 40, 'mastering-suite.tag3' => 40, 'mastering-suite.tag4' => 40, 'mastering-suite.tag5' => 40, 'studio-cta.title' => 80, 'studio-cta.desc' => 300, 'studio-cta.btn' => 40, ``` --- ## PART 2: EQUIPMENT PAGE ### 2A — Problem The category section headers (label, title, count) in each equipment category are hardcoded static strings in the CMS editor. They are visible text in the frontend but cannot be edited. Fix: add an editable box for each category's header, and update equipment.php to use `c()` for those strings. **Categories and their current hardcoded texts in equipment.php:** 1. Konsolen & Recording: label="Konsolen & Recording", title="Das Herzstück", count="3 Einheiten" 2. Mikrofone: label="Mikrofone", title="Vintage & Modern", count="12+ Mikrofone" 3. Outboard: label="Outboard Gear", title="EQ · Kompression · Sättigung", count="20+ Einheiten" 4. Monitoring: label="Monitoring", title="Die Wahrheit hören", count="3 Systeme" 5. Instrumente: label="Instrumente", title="Spielbereit", count="Studio-Inventory" ### 2B — Changes to `equipment.php` For each `
` block in equipment.php, replace the hardcoded label/title/count with c() calls. The key pattern is `{category}-header.label`, `{category}-header.title`, `{category}-header.count`. Find each `cat-header` div and change hardcoded text to use CMS: ```php // Konsolen section (around line 411-417):

... // Mikrofone (around line 488):

// Outboard (around line 616):

// Monitoring (around line 746):

// Instrumente (around line 819):

``` ### 2C — Changes to `cms/index.php` Equipment section For each category section in the CMS equipment editor, replace the static header (`
...
...
`) with an editable box containing label, title, count. For example, replace: ```php
Konsolen & Recording
Das Herzstück
``` With: ```php
``` Do the same for all 5 category sections (konsolen, mikrofone, outboard, monitoring, instrumente) with their correct labels/titles/counts and their correct section background class (--card / --dark alternating as in the current code). ### 2D — Add $limits for equipment header fields ```php 'konsolen-header.label' => 50, 'konsolen-header.title' => 60, 'konsolen-header.count' => 30, 'mikrofone-header.label' => 50, 'mikrofone-header.title' => 60, 'mikrofone-header.count' => 30, 'outboard-header.label' => 50, 'outboard-header.title' => 60, 'outboard-header.count' => 30, 'monitoring-header.label' => 50, 'monitoring-header.title' => 60, 'monitoring-header.count' => 30, 'instrumente-header.label' => 50, 'instrumente-header.title' => 60, 'instrumente-header.count' => 30, ``` --- ## IMPORTANT NOTES 1. Read the actual current file content before any edit. The exact line numbers in the description are approximate — find the correct location by reading. 2. In cms/index.php, the `section` wrapper for each equipment category currently wraps both the header AND the item grid in one `
`. When you add a separate box for the header, close the original section properly and open a new one for the item grid. 3. In studio.php, be careful with the room card structures — the Live Room card is `room-card--full` with two columns in the body div. 4. Preserve all existing CSS classes and HTML structure. 5. After editing, confirm what was changed.

attachment.content:
- anthropic-skills:code-reviewer: Complete toolkit for automated code review with modern tools and best practices. Use this skill whenever the user wants to review code, analyze a pull request (PR), check code quality, generate a review report, detect anti-patterns, enforce coding standards, or audit a codebase for security/performance/maintainability issues. Trigger even for casual requests like "look at my code", "check this PR", "is this code good?", "find bugs in my file", or "what's wrong with this function". Covers TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin, React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and more. Always activate for any code quality, review, or analysis workflow.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:gdpr-dsgvo-expert: Senior GDPR/DSGVO expert and internal/external auditor for data protection compliance. Provides EU GDPR and German DSGVO expertise, privacy impact assessments, data protection auditing, and compliance verification. Use for GDPR compliance assessments, privacy audits, data protection planning, and regulatory compliance verification.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:senior-backend: Complete toolkit for senior backend engineers using modern tools and best practices. Use this skill whenever the user wants to build, scaffold, migrate, test, or optimize backend systems — including APIs, databases, microservices, authentication, security, or DevOps pipelines. Trigger even for casual requests like "help me set up a REST API", "how do I migrate my database", "load test my endpoint", "review my backend architecture", "optimize my queries", or "deploy my Node.js app". Also activate for any backend code review, security audit, or infrastructure task involving TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Node.js, Express, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, Prisma, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, or Azure.
- anthropic-skills:legal-impressum-germany: German Impressum requirements under DDG and MStV. All Rechtsformen including GmbH, AG, UG, GbR, OHG, KG.
- anthropic-skills:senior-frontend: Comprehensive frontend development skill for building modern, performant web applications using React, Next.js, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Use this skill whenever the user asks to build frontend features, create React or Next.js components, optimize bundle size or performance, implement UI/UX designs, manage state (Zustand, Redux, React Query), review frontend code, scaffold new projects, or troubleshoot frontend issues. Also trigger for TypeScript type design, accessibility audits, and responsive layout work. Activate even for casual requests like "help me build a component", "review my React code", or "why is my Next.js app slow".
- anthropic-skills:senior-security
- anthropic-skills:ui-ux-pro-max: UI/UX design intelligence with 50+ styles, 97 color palettes, 57 font pairings, and 9 tech stacks (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, Tailwind, shadcn/ui). Use this skill whenever the user wants to design, build, create, implement, review, fix, improve, optimize, enhance, or refactor any UI — even if they don't say 'design'. Triggers include: website, landing page, dashboard, admin panel, e-commerce, SaaS, portfolio, blog, mobile app, .html/.tsx/.vue/.svelte files, and individual elements like button, modal, navbar, sidebar, card, table, form, chart. Also triggers for style keywords: glassmorphism, claymorphism, minimalism, brutalism, neumorphism, bento grid, dark mode, responsive. And for topics: color palette, accessibility, animation, layout, typography, font pairing, hover effects, gradients.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- anthropic-skills:pptx
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review
